steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Move the constant to the other side, then add $9$ to both sides because half of $-6$ is $-3$ and $(-3)^2=9$.
$$x^2-6x=4$$
$$x^2-6x+9=4+9$$
$$(x-3)^2=13$$

steps.title: Match the radical form
steps.content:
Take the square root and solve for $x$.
$$x-3=\pm\sqrt{13}$$
$$x=3\pm\sqrt{13}$$
One solution has the form $3+\sqrt{k}$, so compare $3+\sqrt{13}$ with $3+\sqrt{k}$. The answer is B.
